description: Native golden scenario manifest for M5 substrate proof. Maps alignment scenarios (D1-D15) to deterministic subpipeline native trace coverage. Committed scenarios have hermetic native runs producing multi-file trace directories; deferred scenarios lack deterministic no-service runners and are documented for M6/report-conformance.

deterministic_runners.coverage: Exercises native prep/plan stages with human gate suspension and plan artifact generation. Covers blocking_questions_suspend, resume_clarify, and imported_criteria_declared_outputs cases.
Exercises simple linear prep-to-emit native pipeline with artifact output.
Exercises ingest/audit/emit native pipeline with deterministic fake worker.

committed.substrate_note: Substrate proof only: validates native runtime stages, event kinds, artifact outputs, and checkpoint generation. Report conformance for Prep/Plan product semantics deferred to M6.

deterministic_runners.coverage: Exercises critique_creative and revise_creative stages with structured native program instructions. Covers bare_skip and sequential_fallback cases.

committed.substrate_note: Substrate proof only: validates native critique stage dispatch and trace event sequence. Parallel lens fanout and evaluator retry require LLM inference — not covered by deterministic runners.

deterministic_runners.coverage: Exercises human gate with suspend/resume, validating that native suspension and resume paths are structurally correct. Covers suspension_kind and resume_cursor contract.

committed.substrate_note: Substrate proof only: validates native suspension mechanism and resume cursor contract. Malformed/empty payload recovery and agent routing require LLM — not covered by deterministic runners.

deterministic_runners.coverage: Exercises critique→revise→finalize loop in native program. Covers flag_resolution_success and iterate_to_revise cases through structured stage sequence.
Exercises panel_review→synth→revise→human_decide loop with native resume. Covers human gate suspension within revise loop.

committed.substrate_note: Substrate proof only: validates native revise loop stage sequencing and resume after human gate. Flag debt acceptance, reprompt, and typed outcomes require LLM — not covered by deterministic runners.

deterministic_runners.coverage: Exercises parallel candidate scoring, pairwise bracket, and winner selection. Covers researcher_branch, challenger_branch, and pick_promotes_decision cases.
Exercises classify→diagnose→repair_decision→recheck_emit stages. Covers decision resolution with structured native output.

committed.substrate_note: Substrate proof only: validates native parallel fanout, bracket reduction, and decision emission. Escalate-to-human and replan routes require LLM — not covered by deterministic runners.

deterministic_runners.coverage: Exercises finalize stage with artifact output (v1.md). Covers task_generation and artifact_contract cases.
Exercises assembly finalization with parallel section drafts feeding into final.md. Covers finalize with dependency ordering.
Exercises final_report stage after multi-layer panel synthesis. Covers user_actions_declared and artifact_contract cases.

committed.substrate_note: Substrate proof only: validates native finalize stage artifact output and trace contract. Baseline selection, failure-to-revise, and before-execute gate require LLM — not covered by deterministic runners.

deterministic_runners.coverage: Exercises parallel section_drafts fanout (dependency-ordered batch execution). Covers dependency_ordering and runtime_task_batch_map cases.
Exercises parallel candidate scoring (candidate_score_0/1/2) with dependency on pairwise_bracket reduction. Covers partial_failure_resume contract.
Exercises three-layer parallel panel fanout (layer_high/mid/low_panel + synth). Covers dependency_edges_visible and task_paths_stable cases.

committed.substrate_note: Substrate proof only: validates native parallel fanout execution, dependency ordering, and batch stage sequencing. Task complexity model routing requires LLM — not covered by deterministic runners.

deterministic_runners.coverage: Exercises human_decide gate with suspension (choices: continue/stop) and resume with human_input='stop'. Covers approval_approve, approval_deny, and approval_resume cases.
Exercises human_gate stage with structured native suspension. Covers deferred_human_verify contract.

committed.substrate_note: Substrate proof only: validates native human gate suspension/resume mechanism and resume cursor contract. No-review-to-done and protected_action_gate require full product pipeline — not covered by deterministic runners.

deterministic_runners.coverage: Every subpipeline test produces a complete native trace directory (events.ndjson, state.json, stages.json, artifacts.json, checkpoint.json). Covers tree_trace_snapshot, replay_consistency, and resume_from_path cases. The GoldenRegressionRule with directory digest and .explanation.md sidecar enforces unreviewed_golden_rebaseline_rejected.

committed.substrate_note: Substrate proof only: validates native trace emission contract (5 canonical files), trace normalization (seq/ts_utc/ts_rel_init_s stripping), directory-level golden regression with explanation-sidecar guard, and checkpoint-driven resume. This is the core D12 proof — golden trace regeneration and semantic diff review are enforced by infrastructure, not product semantics.

deferred.reason: Requires LLM inference for parallel review checks. No deterministic no-service runner exists for review content evaluation.
composition_visible_policy_checks: Review fanout and reducer visible as parallel_map node in compiled topology
Review check children visible in static snapshot with deterministic ordering
REVIEW_POLICY metadata includes parallel check children and reducer routes

deferred.reason: Requires LLM inference for review approval/block/rework decisions and cap-exhaustion logic. No deterministic no-service runner exists.
composition_visible_policy_checks: Review rework loop with approve/request-rework/block/force-proceed/escalate outcomes visible
Cap exhaustion outcome classifier visible outside retained handler
REVIEW_POLICY metadata includes rework loop bounds, cap outcomes, and escalation routes

deferred.reason: Requires external human interaction and override action dispatch. No deterministic no-service runner exists for human control surface.
composition_visible_policy_checks: Override action matrix with all 11 action keys and route/effect classification visible
Human suspension points with durable resume coordinates declared in workflow source
OVERRIDE_POLICY metadata on compiled workflow includes full action matrix routes

deferred.reason: Requires timeout/deadline infrastructure, credential policy gates, and worker lease management. These are platform-level concerns, not runtime trace concerns.
composition_visible_policy_checks: Timeout/deadline policy declared at step/subworkflow call sites in workflow source
Model routing visible as declared policy keyed by phase/task complexity
Rendered policy view shows EXECUTE_POLICY, REVIEW_POLICY, GATE_POLICY, OVERRIDE_POLICY metadata attached to compiled workflow
Exported constants alone rejected as insufficient proof of policy conformance

deferred.reason: Compiler-level validation of DSL authoring contracts, not a runtime trace scenario. Verified through pipeline contract tests (test_pipeline_contracts.py) and compiler unit tests.
composition_visible_policy_checks: Compiler accepts runtime-list parallel_map with dynamic items
Compiler accepts nested workflow invocation with literal call_site_id
Compiler accepts typed loop outcomes or safe break/continue subset
Compiler/harness rejects direct manifest authoring bypass
Accepted shadow topology exists before migration with reviewer signoff

deferred.reason: Handler purity audit and source-path reconciliation. Not a runtime trace scenario — verified through handler extraction tools and source inventory.
composition_visible_policy_checks: Handler purity scan covers current_state, next_step, workflow_transition, run_parallel, auto-loop dispatch, override action dispatch
Mutation tests moving semantics back into handlers cause conformance failure
Installed package source paths match canonical inventory with stale-path negative check
Scenario goldens cover all named paths for behavior parity verification

contract.normalization: events.ndjson: strip seq, ts_utc, ts_rel_init_s; all other files: canonical JSON comparison

north_star_note: This manifest captures native substrate proof only. Final report conformance for visible Megaplan product semantics (including deferred D9-D11 and D13-D15) is owned by composition M6. Committed golden traces prove native runtime behavior (dispatch, fanout, suspension, resume, checkpoint) — not product-level planning correctness.
